success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
High import feasibility (0.91) and low intrusiveness risk enabled adapter_mode=import without invasive code changes.
Generated MCP service started successfully with healthy service status over stdio transport.
Strong upstream project structure and packaging metadata (pyproject.toml + setup.cfg + setup.py) supported automated analysis and wrapping.
Clear module/API discovery from core Chempy domains (stoichiometry, equilibria, kinetics, parsing, units) enabled broad endpoint coverage.
failure_reasons: Quality-gate gap: original_project tests show passed=false with empty details and execution_time=0, so upstream compatibility was not validated in this run.
Observability gap: performance/resource metrics are zero/empty, preventing runtime efficiency verification.
Tooling signal mismatch: plugin_generation reports tool_endpoints=0 while service info lists 23 endpoints, indicating reporting inconsistency.
DeepWiki enrichment failed (non-blocking), reducing external analysis depth.

node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed, but endpoint-level and upstream project tests were effectively not executed/recorded.
